text: The glowing blue light behind the glass fills me with a sense of technological isolation. Here, in this quiet, sterile corridor, my footsteps echo in the empty space. I feel as though I stand at a threshold—not only to a room, but to some liminal territory between creation and critique, invention and dissatisfaction. The sign on the wall, 'R + D,' reinforces a theme of experimentation and ongoing inquiry. I am curious, and yet, I also feel the weight of expectation and the quiet hum of unresolved questions, as if the innovations in this space cannot escape the criticisms awaiting outside. It is a place for reflection, but also for confrontation with the imperfect reality of what we build.
